California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornrnission
January 2, L992
GIen H. Spain
Attorney at Lar^/
P-O. Box 205
comptche, cA 95427
RE: Your Request for Advice
our Fil"e No. A-91-534
Dear Mr. Spain:
You trave requested advice on behalf of Forests Forever, Inc.
(the 'rcorporation") and its sponsored ball-ot measure committee,
Forests Forever l,Iovember '92 (the I'Committee" ) , regarding the
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
,,Act,, ) . 1/
OUESTIONS
1. 
Does the Act's def initi.on of "contribution" incfude
Iegal services provided by you to the corporation and the
committee and related rnisce]laneous expenses if paynent for the
services and expenses will be deferred until sufficient funds have
been raised?
2. 
If the answer to Question 1 is yes, are such
contributions disclosed as "in-ki.ndrt loans?
3. 
If the corporation rnakes a repayment on an in-kind loan
on beharf of the committee, is the repayment a contribution?
4. 
Does the answer to Question 3 differ if both the
Corporation and the Committee are jointly liable for repayment of
the in-kind loan?
l/ 
covernment code Sections 810oo-91015. AII' statutory
references are to the Government code unless otheri+ise indicated.
Comrnission regulations appear at 2 Calj,fornia Code of Regulations
Section rS0oo, et seq. AIl references to requlations are to TitLe
2, Division 6 c:- the California Code of Regulations.

Glen H. Spain
No. A-9■-534
Page 2
coNctusroNa
l-. 
LegaI services provided to and related expenses incurred
on behal-f of the committee for which payment wifl be made at a
later date are contributions if the committee has begun
circulation of the signature petitions to qual-ify the measure for
the baLl,ot. Legal expenses rel,ated to drafting the petition and
qualifying the measure for the ballot whj.ch were incurred prior to
the tirne signature petitions are circulated, are neither
reportable contributions nor expenditures.
2. 
Yes. A contribution in the form of goods or services
for which payment wilL be made at a later date must be disclosed
as an tt in-kindrr loan.
3. 
The corporation's repayment of an in-kind loan on behal.f
of the committee is a contribution- 
The reporting of such
contributions is discussed in the Analysis below.
4. 
The ansvrer to Question 3 does not change if the
Corporat j.on and the committee are jointly liable for repayment of
the loan.
FACTS
You are the general- counsel for Forests Forever, Inc., a
nonprofit political 
action organization. one of the prirnary
purposes of this organization is to draft stateeride ballot
initiatives 
and to organize and manage canpaigns for those
statewide ballot inj.tiatives it has drafted. 
You recently drafted
a statewide baltot measure and submitted it for filj-ng. 
fn
addition, you recently formed a recipient committee entitled
ItForests Forever Novenber '92 committee.I' The Connittee is a
ballot measure committee sponsored by Forests Forever, Inc.
Your services, prior to the formation of the comnittee,
included drafting the ba1lot initiative 
and processing the
required legaI documents through the appropriate governmental-
agencies for filing with the Secretary of State. These services
were considered to be joint obligations of the Corporation and the
Comrnittee and payable by either.
Future services you will provide to the comnittee incl-ude
revj.ewing i.ts reports and filings, 
providing legaI advice w.ith
respect to campaign issues, and litigation 
work whi.ch rnay arise
during the course of the campaign. AlL of these services will 
be
billable to the committee but with payment guaranteed by the
Corporation if the committee's funds are insufficient to pay the
obligations.


c len tl . Spa in
No- A-91-534
Page 3
ANALYSIS
A ,'contr ibut ion,' is a payment made for political 
purposes.
(Section 82015.) Regulation 18215 further defines " contribution, r'
in pertinent part, as follows:
(a) A contribution is any monetary or
nonrnonetary payment made for political 
purposes for
which fuII and adequate consideration is not made
to the donor. A payment is made for political
purposes if it is:
(2) Received by or made at the behest of:
(D) An organization formed or existing
pr5-marity for political 
purposes. . . .
Goods and services provided to the committee for which full
and adequate consideration is not paid within the normal course of
businesJ are contributions. 
(sectj-on 82o15.) such contributions
are disclosed as "in-kind" foans if pa)4llent for the goods or
services wil-I be made at a tater date. A contribution in the form
of an in-kind }oan is discLosed on Schedufe C of the Form 419
(Ballot Measure Committee Campaign Statenent) filed for the period
in which the services were provided. If a payment is made on the
Ioan during that reporting period, the pa),ment is disclosed on
Schedule E. If pa)rment is not made during the reporting period,
disclose the in-kind loan as an accrued expense on ScheduLe F.
certain palrments incurred in connection wi.th quali-fying a
measure for the ballot are not contributions or rePortable
campaj.gn expenditures under the Act. 
(Sections 82015 and 82025.)
tn the-rontina opinion (Lg76, 2 FPPC ops. 25),3/ th" cornrnission
determined that expenditures for legal expenses incurred prior to
the tine petitions are circulated to qualify a measure for the
baIlot are neither expenditures nor contributions. 
(Beattv Advice
Memo, No. . T-87-092. )
3/
in
and
at
Copies of Cornmission regulations and opinions are available
many Ia\,, I j.braries. Alternatively, copies of these materials
Commissi.on advice Ietters may be pbtained from the Conmission
t1 cost of 10C per page.


Glen H. Spa in
No. A-91-5 3 4
Page 4
AII payments made at the behest of the Committee are
contributj.ons, including loan repayments made by the Corporation
on the Committee,s behalf. 
(Section 82015, Regutation LA?LS,
supra, and Seelv Advice Letter, No. A-86-269.) This advice does
not change if the Committee and the Corporation are jointly
responsible for payment of a debt. Therefore, any debt for which
the Connittee is either fully or jointly responsible must be
disclosed on the Committee,s campaign discloiure statement.
In any reporting per j-od i-n which the Corporation rnakes a
payment on behalf of the Connittee, the Comnittee nust disclose
the Corporation,s payment as a monetary contribution on Schedule A
and as an expenditure on Schedule E, or a payment of an accrued.
expense on Schedule F, whichever is applicable. A notation should
be made on the campaign statement indicating that the expenditure
was made fron the Corporationrs account.
Please do not hesitate to contact rne at (916) 322-5662 if you
have additionaJ. questions.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral Counsel
9

lA -x),-/) ,-.* .--a-n- u )--,,/
UcLol
By: Lynda Cassady '
Political 
Reform consultant
